concerns.text: Task T012 specifies downloading from 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/GFA-D/pilot_flags', but spec FR-001 and Assumptions explicitly mandate 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/GFA-D2/pilot_flags'. This URL mismatch violates the constitutional requirement for a canonical source (Constitution Principle I) and will cause the pipeline to fetch the wrong dataset or fail.

Task T032a implements DoA using a 'Convex Hull' directly on the feature space. However, Plan.md (Phase 3 Step 3) and Complexity Tracking explicitly mandate 'PCA reduction before DoA to handle high dimensions' to prevent false flags. The task omits this required dimensionality reduction step, violating the plan's technical approach for the DoA check.

Task T032b introduces a 'PCA/Mahalanobis fallback' as a primary execution path if T032a fails. The spec (FR-009) mandates a convex hull approach and does not authorize PCA as an alternative DoA method. Introducing this fallback without explicit spec authorization constitutes a scope violation and potential drift from the required scientific method.

Task T035 describes training 'multiple models' and saving them as 'ensemble_{i}.pkl (i=0..9)' but fails to explicitly enforce the count of 10 models required by spec FR-003 ('bootstrapped ensemble of 10 RandomForest models'). This ambiguity risks a mismatch in the number of ensemble members, violating the specific requirement for confidence interval estimation.

Task T034 introduces a conditional fallback to an absolute cutoff '< 4.0' if the percentile calculation fails. Spec FR-006 requires filtering based on the 'lower tail of the dataset distribution' (10th percentile) and does not define this arbitrary fallback behavior. This allows the system to use an unverified threshold instead of the data-driven requirement, violating the strict filtering logic.
